behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a local module boundary (diff parser/scoper -> review-input builder), not single-function logic only. Real OS/network/subprocess is not required, and correctness does not depend on model output quality. Rejected System: behavior text only requires scoping correctness, not real HTTP/PR API behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no requirement about model judgment or multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire diff-ingestion and input-construction modules together with fixture PR diffs containing unchanged context and multiple hunks; assert generated review payload includes only changed-line snippets and excludes unchanged file regions.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary is real GitHub diff-position semantics (line/side/commit mapping) over real HTTP. In-process fakes can hide mismatches that only appear against the live PR diff renderer/API contract. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior is positional API correctness, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed diff coordinates and verify via API/UI that each comment lands on the intended changed line/hunk.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment (detecting an actual bug). A stubbed/model-mocked test would always pass/fail by fixture and cannot validate defect-finding quality. Rejected Workflow: behavior asks for one detection outcome, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run analysis with the real model on a PR fixture containing a seeded known bug; assert at least one returned finding correctly targets that bug (or its direct symptom).

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module state handoff (inline post results -> summary assembler) and run-consistency logic. It does not require real OS/network if API responses are represented faithfully in-process. Rejected System: behavior text is about ID correspondence from same run, not live API transport behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute posting + summary-construction modules with deterministic fixtures for inline post responses (comment IDs) and verify summary entries reference only IDs produced in that exact run.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in pipeline handoff/aggregation across local modules (findings collection -> summary payload builder). No real OS/network/subprocess is inherently required to expose a dropped-item bug. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on pre-post payload completeness, not external API effects. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the aggregation path with a multi-finding fixture set (including edge cases like duplicate paths or long text) and assert one-to-one preservation of all inline findings into the final summary payload.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real pull request and live sequence execution over external API/network. Mocking can hide real contract/order/HTTP failures. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: findings are fixed precomputed, so correctness does not depend on model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run the end-to-end pipeline against a real PR using a fixed findings fixture; verify diff read succeeds, all inline comments are posted, and the final summary is posted without manual steps.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This run depends on real model-generated findings; stubs cannot validate whether model output is suitable for downstream posting/summarization. Real PR/API is also involved, but System alone would miss model-dependent failures. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic decision stage (analysis) is described; downstream steps are deterministic posting/orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full unattended run on a real PR with the real model enabled; verify model returns actionable findings, inline posts succeed from those findings, and summary posts with consistent linkage.
